```spthy
theory CertificationPath_SignEncrypt
begin

/* 
  Protocol: Certification-path based signed token with confidential payload

  Roles:
    - A (originator/signer): Generates a fresh nonce rA and timestamp tA,
      prepares a token Info = <tA, rA, B, sgnData, Bp[encData]>, signs h(Info) with As,
      and sends: (B --> A), A{Info}  where we model (B --> A) by including Ap.
    - B (recipient/verifier): Obtains Ap from the included certification path,
      verifies the signature on h(Info), checks that B is the intended recipient,
      and decrypts Bp[encData] using Bs.

  Cryptographic primitives:
    - signing (sign/verify)
    - asymmetric-encryption (aenc/adec)
    - hashing (h)

  Intended security goals:
    - Confidentiality of encData: Only B (holding Bs) should learn encData.
      Secrecy is conditioned on no long-term key reveal of Bs.
    - Authentication (non-injective) of A to B on the token contents:
      If B accepts a token, then A must have sent a matching token, unless As is compromised.
    - Executability: There exists a trace where B successfully accepts a token.
*/

builtins: signing, asymmetric-encryption, hashing

/* Role A: constructs and signs the token, and encrypts confidential part for B */
let A(~As, pBs, pAs) =
  new rA;                      // fresh anti-replay nonce
  new tA;                      // timestamp (modeled as fresh)
  new sgnData;                 // non-confidential parameters (integrity only)
  new encData;                 // confidential parameters (to be kept secret from attacker)
  event AGen(rA, tA);
  event SecretForB(pBs, encData);   // mark secrecy target bound to B's public key
  let encForB = aenc(encData, pBs) in
  let info    = <tA, rA, pBs, sgnData, encForB> in     // include B's identity as pBs
  let sig     = sign(h(info), ~As) in                  // A{Info} = <Info, As[h(Info)]>
  event ASent(pAs, pBs, tA, rA, sgnData, encData);
  // Send certification path B-->A (idealized here by including Ap = pAs), and the signed token
  out(<'pathBA', pAs, <info, sig>>);
  0

/* Role B: verifies path to obtain Ap, checks signature and recipient, decrypts encData */
let B(~Bs) =
  in(msg);
  let <='pathBA', pAsC, <info, sig>> = msg in
  let <tA, rA, pBs_in, sgnData, encForB> = info in
  // Check this token is addressed to B
  if pBs_in = pk(~Bs) then
    // Verify A's signature against the obtained Ap from the certification path
    if verify(sig, h(info), pAsC) = true then
      // Decrypt the confidential payload
      let decData = adec(encForB, ~Bs) in
      event BAccept(pAsC, pk(~Bs), tA, rA, sgnData, decData)
    else 0
  else 0

/* Long-term key reveal hooks to model compromise */
let LeakA(~As, pAs) =
  event LtkReveal(pAs);
  out(~As)

let LeakB(~Bs, pBs) =
  event LtkReveal(pBs);
  out(~Bs)

/* One-time setup: generate long-term keys for A and B, publish public parts, run roles */
process:
  new ~As; let pAs = pk(~As) in
  new ~Bs; let pBs = pk(~Bs) in
  out(pAs); out(pBs);                        // public keys are public knowledge
  ( !A(~As, pBs, pAs)
  | !B(~Bs)
  | LeakA(~As, pAs)                          // optional compromise of A's signing key
  | LeakB(~Bs, pBs)                          // optional compromise of B's decryption key
  )

/* Lemmas */

/* Executability: there exists a run where B accepts a token */
lemma executability:
  exists-trace
  "Ex pAs pBs tA rA sgnData v #i.
     BAccept(pAs, pBs, tA, rA, sgnData, v) @ #i"

/* Confidentiality of encData: unless B's long-term key is revealed, the attacker
   never learns the plaintext encData designated for B. */
lemma confidentiality_encData:
"
  not(
    Ex pBs v #i #j.
      SecretForB(pBs, v) @ #i
      & K(v) @ #j
      & not(Ex #r. LtkReveal(pBs) @ #r)
  )
"

/* Authentication of A to B (non-injective):
   If B accepts a token, then A sent a matching token earlier,
   unless A's long-term signing key was revealed. */
lemma authenticity_A_to_B:
"
  All pAs pBs tA rA sgnData v #i.
    BAccept(pAs, pBs, tA, rA, sgnData, v) @ #i
    ==>
      (Ex #j. ASent(pAs, pBs, tA, rA, sgnData, v) @ #j & #j < #i)
      | (Ex #r. LtkReveal(pAs) @ #r)
"

end
```

      and decrypts Bp[encData] using Bs.

    - signing (sign/verify)
    - asymmetric-encryption (aenc/adec)
    - hashing (h)

    - Confidentiality of encData: Only B (holding Bs) should learn encData.
      Secrecy is conditioned on no long-term key reveal of Bs.
    - Authentication (non-injective) of A to B on the token contents:
      If B accepts a token, then A must have sent a matching token, unless As is compromised.
    - Executability: There exists a trace where B successfully accepts a token.
*/

builtins: signing, asymmetric-encryption, hashing

  new encData;                 // confidential parameters (to be kept secret from attacker)
  event AGen(rA, tA);

  let sig     = sign(h(info), ~As) in                  // A{Info} = <Info, As[h(Info)]>

  out(<'pathBA', pAs, <info, sig>>);

let B(~Bs) =
  in(msg);
  let <='pathBA', pAsC, <info, sig>> = msg in
  let <tA, rA, pBs_in, sgnData, encForB> = info in

    else 0
  else 0

/* Long-term key reveal hooks to model compromise */
let LeakA(~As, pAs) =
  event LtkReveal(pAs);
  out(~As)

let LeakB(~Bs, pBs) =
  event LtkReveal(pBs);
  out(~Bs)

/* One-time setup: generate long-term keys for A and B, publish public parts, run roles */
process:
  new ~As; let pAs = pk(~As) in
  new ~Bs; let pBs = pk(~Bs) in

  ( !A(~As, pBs, pAs)
  | !B(~Bs)
  | LeakA(~As, pAs)                          // optional compromise of A's signing key
  | LeakB(~Bs, pBs)                          // optional compromise of B's decryption key
  )

/* Lemmas */

/* Executability: there exists a run where B accepts a token */
lemma executability:
  exists-trace
  "Ex pAs pBs tA rA sgnData v #i.

/* Authentication of A to B (non-injective):
   If B accepts a token, then A sent a matching token earlier,
   unless A's long-term signing key was revealed. */
lemma authenticity_A_to_B:

  All pAs pBs tA rA sgnData v #i.
    BAccept(pAs, pBs, tA, rA, sgnData, v) @ #i
